channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Assam Records 116 Nominations Ahead of Assembly Elections; West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Puducherry Poll Preparations Underway
item.description: As the Assembly elections draw near, Assam has seen a total of 116 candidates filing their nominations, including Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, the Speaker, and the Leader of Opposition, accordi...

item.title: #TukoKadi Youth Drive Voter Registration Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Kenya's youth are making waves with the #TukoKadi movement, a viral campaign pushing for mass voter registration ahead of the 2027 General Elections. Started by young people on social media, it all be...

item.title: DSWD to Hold Special Payout for Unserved NCR Tricycle Drivers Next Week
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) announced that it will conduct a special payout next week for tricycle drivers in the National Capital Region (NCR) who were unable to receive t...

item.title: UK and French navies intercept sanctioned Russian oil tanker in the Mediterranean
item.description: British and French naval forces have carried out a joint operation in the Mediterranean Sea to intercept a Russian-linked oil tanker, as part of efforts to curb Moscow’s so-called 'shadow fleet' used ...

item.title: Trump signals possible de‑escalation of U.S. military campaign in Iran while leaving ground options open
item.description: President Donald Trump has said the United States is considering winding down its military efforts in Iran, telling supporters that Washington is close to achieving key objectives after nearly four we...

item.title: Thunderstorms cause heavy rainfall and water accumulation in Karachi
item.description: Karachi experienced heavy rain along with thunder and lightning across several key areas including Malir, Shah Faisal Colony, Saddar, and II Chundrigar Road. Intermittent showers continued throughout ...

item.title: US Court Blocks Pentagon's Restrictions on Press Access
item.description: A US federal judge has given a big thumbs down to the Pentagon's attempt to tighten the rules about who gets access to press briefings and information. The court ruled that the new policy, which essen...

item.title: King Gustaf and Ambassador Matinyi Discuss Continued Tanzania–Sweden Cooperation as Envoy Ends Tenure
item.description: In Stockholm, King Carl XVI Gustaf of Sweden officially bid farewell to Tanzania’s outgoing ambassador, Mobhare Matinyi, during a short ceremony at the Royal Palace. The meeting highlighted the strong...

item.title: Tshwane suspends MMC Kholofelo Morodi following allegations of tender document leak revealed at Madlanga inquiry
item.description: The City of Tshwane has suspended its Member of the Mayoral Committee (MMC) for Corporate and Shared Services, Kholofelo Morodi, after evidence emerged at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry suggesting...

item.title: Tanzania Government Mediates Amazonite Gemstone Mining Dispute in Karatu
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Tanzanian government, through Deputy Minister for Minerals Dr Steven Kiruswa, has stepped in to settle a disagreement over the Amazonite gemstone mining at Mandagawa Hamlet, Quande...

item.title: President Marcos emphasizes values of compassion and unity beyond Eid'l Fitr
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. encouraged Filipinos to embody the values of compassion, humility, and solidarity not only during Ramadan and Eid'l Fitr but throughout the year to help build...

item.title: Côte d’Ivoire’s new agriculture minister focuses on strengthening cocoa sector amid crisis
item.description: Bruno Nabagné Koné, who recently took over as Côte d’Ivoire’s Minister of Agriculture, Rural Development, and Food Production, is stepping into his new role at a time when the nation’s vital cocoa ind...

item.title: Granting legal rights to rivers could strengthen environmental protection against pollution
item.description: World Water Day on 22 March highlights the ongoing problem of river pollution in the UK, where 450,000 raw sewage discharges were recorded in 2024 alone. Critics link these issues to the privatisation...

item.title: Eastern Cape authorities reject unauthorised installation of Nigerian traditional leader
item.description: The Eastern Cape government has strongly rejected the reported coronation of a Nigerian individual, Chief Solomon Ogbonna Eziko, who was referred to as 'Igwe Ndigbo Na East London'. MEC for Co-operati...

item.title: Kolkata Man Dies in Hospital Lift After Being Trapped for Over an Hour
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded at the R.G. Kar Medical College and Hospital in Kolkata where a 40‑year‑old man, identified as Arup Bandopadhyay, lost his life after being trapped inside a malfunctioning e...

item.title: Burundian Refugees Return Home Voluntarily Ahead of Nyarugusu and Nduta Camp Closures
item.description: Thousands of Burundian refugees living in Nduta camp in Kibondo District and Nyarugusu camp in Kasulu District, Kigoma Region, Tanzania, have continued returning to their home country, Burundi, on vol...

item.title: Manchester United and Bournemouth Play Out 2-2 Draw in EPL Thriller
item.description: For Friday night EPL match, Manchester United and Bournemouth knack waka enter better drama as dem share points 2-2. Bruno Fernandes shine gobe, score penalty plus small help with own goal wey put Man...

item.title: DOH plans to buy nearly 7 million flu vaccines for priority groups
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) announced plans to procure approximately 6.9 million doses of influenza vaccines for 2026, aiming to protect vulnerable populations such as senior citizens and health wo...

item.title: Alex Mwakideu Shares His Views on Gender Roles in Marriage
item.description: Radio presenter and YouTuber Alex Mwakideu recently shared his perspective on marriage and household responsibilities, sparking discussion online. He stated that once a man is married, he should not e...

item.title: Poll shows One Nation gaining strong ground in South Australian politics
item.description: A recent poll in South Australia suggests that One Nation is making striking gains in voter support, potentially signalling a broader cultural and political shift in the state. Conducted by research f...
